definition: Grimoire structural recombination grows DTD content models past trivial single-child shapes -- building multi-alternative groups, multi-child SEQ/OR trees, and complete DTD+body skeletons -- reaching validation paths cmplog byte mutations cannot construct.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural Grimoire mutators grow the DTD content model past two children by recombining generalized token/gap structures, triggering the separator-loop re-entry that cmplog's byte-level havoc rarely reaches.
signature.one_line: Grimoire grows DTD content model past two children to trigger separator-loop re-entry

analysis.summary_one_line: A multi-element DTD content model whose separator loop runs twice then hits a non-separator/non-close token after a second child is parsed.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same deep multi-child content model, but reached by splicing/recombining generalised XML/DTD token structures rather than by gradient climbing; the input must still parse two children and re-enter the separator loop on an invalid token.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolved 8/10 with avg hitcount 12 on the true side; the winning seed is a well-formed '<?xml version="1.0"?>\n<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1' skeleton, and GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators recombine such generalised token runs to grow valid nested content models far past where byte-level havoc stalls.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolved only 2/10 and blocked 8/10; its mutators (havoc/token + I2S) mutate at the byte level without generalised structural recombination, so it rarely reproduces a content model deep enough to parse a second child and re-enter the separator loop.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The single-technique delta grimoire vs cmplog (both have I2S) is grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators: string-based grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures builds the deep DTD content model; cmplog lacks this structural stage, so the structural recombination, not I2S, is the cause.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the error arm of the content-model separator loop, taken when the next token is neither ',' nor '|' nor ')'.
Taking the true side requires `last` to be non-NULL, i.e. a second child must have been parsed.
`last` is set when a second element child is parsed in the loop body.
value_profile_cmplog resolves this branch 10/10 while cmplog blocks 8/10, with the winner hitting the true side and the loser never doing so.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=6  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The single-technique delta for pair 1 is value_profile (CMP_MAP gradient on top of cmplog).
value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets over the cmplog stack.
The single-technique delta for pair 2 is grimoire_structural, and grimoire adds generalization plus Grimoire mutators on top of cmplog which already has I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators recombine generalised token structures grammar-free.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire resolves the branch 8/10 with a higher true-side hitcount than the loser, while cmplog blocks 8/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=12  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The grimoire winning seed is a well-formed XML/DOCTYPE skeleton suitable for structural recombination into deeper content models.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a single-element content model (no second child, so `last` stays NULL and line 6461 true-side is unreachable) showing value_profile_cmplog and grimoire still beating cmplog would refute the deep-multi-child-structure requirement.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section is dominated by the shared XML header and shows only PARTIAL single-count W vs L differences, so the deep content-model structural divergence is inferred from source/coverage rather than directly visible in the shown seed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators build deeply nested multi-alternative DTD content models ('(a|b|...)') that create a parent->c2 sibling node; cmplog lacks structural recombination and never assembles such a model.
signature.one_line: Grimoire builds nested DTD content models with c2 sibling cmplog cannot reach

analysis.summary_one_line: A DTD <!ELEMENT> content model with a multi-child SEQ/OR group so the freed node is parent->c2, taking the false branch

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program condition: a DTD <!ELEMENT> content model with a multi-child SEQ/OR group whose freed node maps to parent->c2, but reached via structural recombination of generalised DTD token fragments rather than gradient byte search.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves the false branch in 8/10 trials with the highest hitcount on the branch (63), and its GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators splice generalised token/gap structures, letting it recombine '<!ELEMENT name (a|b|...)' fragments into deeply nested multi-alternative content models that create the c2 sibling driving line 1128.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (the single-technique baseline grimoire builds on) blocks 9/10 with hitcount 0; it lacks the generalization + structural recombination, so it cannot assemble the multi-child group and never produces a parent->c2 node to free.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire structural mutators (string-based, grammar-free recombination of generalised token/gap structures) build the deeply nested multi-alternative '(a|b|...)' content model that cmplog cannot, producing a parent->c2 child and the false branch at valid.c:1125.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of 'if (cur == parent->c1)' which assigns parent->c2 = NULL.
The winner-only path executes the else branch parent->c2 = NULL and then re-enters via cur = parent->c2.
The freed node trees come from element-content allocation; the content-model node constructor is exercised far more by the loser, showing the loser builds shallow models.
value_profile_cmplog wins decisive pair 1 over cmplog at prob_div 0.80 with the value_profile delta.
In pair 1 the winner resolves 8/10 with hitcount 6 while the loser blocks 9 with hitcount 0.
value_profile_cmplog's feedback includes CMP_MAP gradient buckets in addition to edge counters.
grimoire wins decisive pair 2 over cmplog at prob_div 0.80 with the grimoire_structural delta.
In pair 2 the winner resolves 8/10 with hitcount 63 while the loser blocks 9 with hitcount 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=63  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage and Grimoire structural mutators on top of the cmplog stack, recombining generalised token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures

evidence_trail.claim: The branch sits in the DTD element-declaration parse chain, reached through xmlParseElementMixedContentDecl which builds OR/SEQ content trees.
The XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_SEQ and XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OR multi-child content types are reached far more by the resolving runs at the free walk switch.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose <!ELEMENT> content model is a single-child (no SEQ/OR group, only c1) tree in which value_profile_cmplog and grimoire still resolve the false branch (parent->c2 = NULL) at the same rate as cmplog, showing the multi-child content-model feature is not what drives the divergence.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The seed bytes only directly show the loser's DTD prefix; the winner-resolving seed bytes do not literally display a multi-alternative '(a|b|...)' group, so the c2-sibling requirement is inferred from the source/coverage overlay rather than seen verbatim in winner bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: Grimoire's GeneralizationStage and structural mutators co-assemble a multi-construct skeleton (XML prolog + DOCTYPE external reference + body with undeclared element name) that resolves to XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED in mixed-content checking; cmplog's I2S fixes scalar comparands but cannot build this multi-construct DTD+body structure.
signature.one_line: Grimoire assembles DTD+body skeleton placing undeclared element in XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED case

analysis.summary_one_line: A structurally valid XML doc with a DOCTYPE/DTD that references an element name having no matching element declaration (etype UNDEFINED).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed XML input deep enough to reach mixed-content checking inside parsed element content while a DTD/DOCTYPE is loaded, where the current element node's name resolves through xmlGetDtdElementDesc to an elemDecl whose etype is XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED (a referenced but never-declared element). This requires a multi-construct skeleton: a valid XML prolog, a <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd"> external-subset reference, plus a body with character data inside an element whose name has no <!ELEMENT> declaration.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winning seeds carry a complete, structurally coherent skeleton: prolog (<?m.?>), <!DOCTYPEa SYSTEM "dtds/127772.dtd">, and a body <b>...; grimoire's generalization+structural recombination splices these token/gap structures so the parser descends through xmlParseCharData -> areBlanks -> xmlIsMixedElement with myDoc and node->name set, and the named element resolves to an UNDEFINED elemDecl, taking the true branch (W marks 3497/3498 and L3499 false; d=1 L3497 shows W T=26 F=0).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog seeds reach the surrounding parser machinery but never satisfy the mixed-content path to the UNDEFINED case: at the blocker d=1 L3497 cmplog (L) shows T=0 F=30, and at the call site d=2 areBlanks L2921 'if (ret == 0) return(1)' the loser takes T=30 (ELEMENT_TYPE_ELEMENT, a declared element) while never producing an undeclared-element reference; cmplog's I2S byte substitution fixes scalar comparands but cannot assemble the multi-construct DTD+body skeleton that places an undeclared element name in content position.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both cmplog and grimoire run the same shared input-to-state comparison-solving stage, so that technique is NOT the delta between them. The grimoire-only delta is the GeneralizationStage (replacing concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders) plus the Grimoire structural mutators that splice and recurse on generalised token/gap structures (string-based, grammar-free structural mutation). This grimoire structural recombination assembles the deep, multi-construct well-formed XML+DTD skeleton that reaches the mixed-element check with an undeclared element name, which the shared scalar comparison-solving stage alone (as in cmplog) cannot build.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is grimoire>cmplog with the single-technique delta grimoire_structural, and both fuzzers share the I2S stage.
The grimoire/cmplog delta is structural, not I2S, because grimoire builds on the full cmplog I2S stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: Grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage and structural mutators that splice generalised token/gap structures.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker requires elemDecl->etype to be XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED, the true branch of the switch.
At the blocker the winner takes the UNDEFINED true side (T=26) while the loser never does (F=30) and instead takes the declared-ELEMENT case.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3497  T=26 F=0  T=0 F=30  case XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning grimoire seeds contain a complete XML prolog plus a DOCTYPE SYSTEM DTD reference and a body element.
Loser cmplog seeds reach the parser but, at the areBlanks call site, resolve to a declared element (ret==0 true) rather than producing an undeclared-element reference.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=2   L2921  T=0 F=26  T=30 F=0  if (ret == 0) return(1);

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which cmplog reaches the XML_ELEMENT_TYPE_UNDEFINED true branch as often as grimoire — i.e., if scalar I2S byte substitution alone, without grimoire's structural recombination, suffices to assemble the well-formed XML+DTD skeleton placing an undeclared element name in content position, then the structural-recombination attribution is wrong.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The winning seeds' recorded mutation_op names the shared comparison-solving stage, not a Grimoire mutator, so the attribution to grimoire's structural recombination rests on the technique delta and the deep multi-construct skeleton rather than a direct lineage trace of a Grimoire mutator producing the find.
